mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative equity returns as investors price in lower corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and industrial production reveals a structural shift in economic momentum. When housing construction accelerates while manufacturing output stagnates, capital reallocates toward cyclical equities, driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain > 5% of forward return variance in a rolling 3-year window, or the signal direction flips consistently across market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth above trend indicates robust supply-side capacity expansion, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month moving average, or the signal exhibits negative predictive power in bear markets.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances measured by RSAFS directly fuel corporate revenue expectations, leading to immediate positive equity price adjustments over the next week.
failure_condition: The rolling Spearman correlation between RSAFS momentum and 5-day SPX returns fails to exceed 0.10 over any 1-year window, or forward returns are flat across all deciles.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.5% signals aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and historically precedes negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power after adjusting for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in employment growth and wage pressure creates a stagflationary risk premium. When nonfarm payrolls surge alongside rising consumer prices, equity valuations contract due to margin compression fears.
failure_condition: The joint distribution of PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative returns in > 60% of high-signal periods, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth combined with sticky inflation erodes consumer purchasing power, triggering a defensive rotation out of equities and generating negative forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when GDP growth decelerates below 1.5% while inflation stays elevated, or the signal exhibits no rank correlation with forward returns in out-of-sample testing.
